You are designing POLARIS's path to the TOP of the DeepResearch Bench board. Read /home/polaris/polaris_project/SOTA_BRIEF_V2.md in full first — every
number in it is measured from primary artifacts or read from the grader's source code.

LINE-BY-LINE STUDY OF THE WINNERS (just completed):
[
 {
  "findings": [
   {
    "what_they_do": "bodhi ends nearly every subsection with an explicitly labeled, citation-free inference sentence that carries NO new facts — 47% of its 148 sentences have no citation marker at all (33 under bolded labels: 'Restructuring implication' x5, 'Restructuring pathway' x4, 'Interpretation' x2, 'Mechanism link', 'Policy tension', 'Implication for restructuring'). These are exactly the sentences that survive the RACE cleaner intact and read as original analysis.",
    "what_we_do": "97.8% of our ~223 sentences terminate in a [n] citation marker — after the cleaner deletes markers, essentially every sentence we ship is a bare reported fact. Our own provenance gate (entailment_judge NEUTRAL clause) deletes precisely bodhi's highest-scoring sentence type. Our only uncited sentences are boilerplate ('This report synthesizes the retrieved research evidence on the question above').",
    "quote_them": "**Restructuring implication**: even when aggregate employment effects are small in the short run, firms may still be actively reorganizing work—shifting hiring away from some roles, rewriting skill requirements, and investing in complementary training and organizational changes.",
    "quote_us": "Every quantitative claim is span-grounded to a cited source; claims that could not be verified against the underlying evidence were removed rather than paraphrased.",
    "criterion": "INSIGHT — 'deep analysis and original insights rather than simply repeating known information' (weight 0.32; our worst-weighted gap, +0.122)",
    "why_it_scores": "The insight rubric literally penalizes 'simply repeating known information'. bodhi devotes nearly half its word budget to interpretation-layer sentences; we devote ~2%. Crucially, bodhi's implication sentences contain no digits and no new proper nouns — they are the sentence type our INTERPRETATION contract already permits, but pointed at adjudication instead of hedging."
   },
   {
    "what_they_do": "bodhi ADJUDICATES conflicting evidence in a fixed three-move pattern: named finding A, named conflicting finding B joined by 'while/however/yet', then a reconciliation sentence stating which conditions produce which outcome. It even grades evidence quality inline in parentheses.",
    "what_we_do": "We juxtapose conflicting numbers with no reconciliation, and our Limitations section explicitly tells the judge we do not adjudicate — a written confession that we fail the 'Critical Evaluation of Evidence and Synthesis of Competing Theories' criterion.",
    "quote_them": "Using an AI occupational impact measure across 23 OECD countries, Georgieff and Hyee report no clear overall relationship between AI exposure and occupational employment growth; however, in occupations with high computer use, greater AI exposure is linked to higher employment growth, and there is suggestive evidence of reduced hours in low-computer-use occupations [[114]](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35620279/).\n\n**Interpretation**: outcomes depend on complementary digital skills and work organization—supporting the complementarity narrative from GPT and organizational-design perspectives",
    "quote_us": "No contradictions were detected by the pipeline, so no cross-source disagreement on magnitude, direction, or endpoint is asserted.",
    "criterion": "INSIGHT — 'Critical Evaluation of Evidence and Synthesis of Competing Theories'; also COMPREHENSIVENESS 'multiple perspectives and balance'",
    "why_it_scores": "The brief says the judge wants ADJUDICATION, not hedging. bodhi also grades causal quality inline: '(This evidence is correlational rather than a clean causal estimate.)' — one sentence of critical scrutiny per conflict. Our synthesis section is another pile of facts, and our Limitations paragraph affirmatively declares we made no adjudications, in the artifact the judge reads."
   },
   {
    "what_they_do": "bodhi makes cross-source inferences constantly — claims no single source supports, welding two named studies into one causal narrative. Roughly half of the 69 uncited sentences (so ~20-25% of ALL its sentences) are this type; the rest are methodological appraisal. It even extrapolates a source beyond its own scope and says so.",
    "what_we_do": "Our gate kills any sentence introducing a mechanism 'NOT present in the SPAN', so we ship only conjunctions. Our closest attempt is a bare 'This finding is consistent with' bridge with no mechanism attached.",
    "quote_them": "yet aggregate impacts on employment and wage growth in exposed occupations/industries were too small to detect over the period studied [[3]](https://econpapers.repec.org/RePEc:ucp:jlabec:doi:10.1086/718327)—a pattern consistent with GPT diffusion lags and within-firm reorganization preceding macro reallocation [[1]](https://dspace.mit.edu/bitstream/handle/1721.1/120302/pandp.20181019.pdf).",
    "quote_us": "This finding is consistent with online labor market evidence that entry-level and less-experienced workers in highly exposed occupations bear the brunt of early displacement effects.[38][39]",
    "criterion": "INSIGHT — 'clear logical reasoning and effectively explains causal relationships behind phenomena'",
    "why_it_scores": "bodhi's inference welds source [3]'s null aggregate result to source [1]'s GPT-lag theory — a mechanism present in NEITHER span, i.e., exactly what our entailment gate deletes. Another: 'While this study is about automation broadly, it provides an institution-based template for studying AI: outcomes likely depend on bargaining structures that shape adjustment margins' — explicit, flagged extrapolation. The judge reads these as causal reasoning; it reads our conjunctions as repetition."
   },
   {
    "what_they_do": "bodhi's 40 H3 headings are full FINDINGS, not topic labels — each heading is itself an adjudicated claim the paragraph then evidences. The heading layer alone reads as a chain of insights.",
    "what_we_do": "Our 11 H2 headings are inert topic labels, and our section-opening sentences are orphaned mid-argument fragments — the literal first sentence of our report body begins with a demonstrative that has no antecedent.",
    "quote_them": "### 3.4 Cross-country evidence: exposure alone does not mechanically predict job loss",
    "quote_us": "Within this framework, automation generates a displacement effect that reduces labor's share of value added, countered by a reinstatement effect as new tasks reinstate labor into a broader range of activities, and the net impact depends on the relative magnitudes of these opposing forces.[1]",
    "criterion": "READABILITY — 'Overall Article Structure and Logical Flow' names 'effective headings and subheadings' (our 0.3774 is the worst score on the board); bleeds into INSIGHT via the judge's forced comparative narrative",
    "why_it_scores": "A judge skimming bodhi's heading tree reads 40 consecutive claims ('Short-run aggregate effects can be small even while firm-level restructuring is visible', 'Wage effects are heterogeneous and technology-type-specific'). Our tree reads 11 labels ('AI, Skills, and Task Transformation'). Our body opens with 'Within this framework' pointing at nothing — the frame was never established. Claim-first headings are free words that score in two dimensions at once."
   },
   {
    "what_they_do": "bodhi's paragraph is a fixed 40-90-word unit: one claim-first topic sentence NAMING the authors in prose, 1-3 evidence sentences with the specific numbers, one bolded implication. 86 paragraphs, median ~38 words. Author names appear in running prose 54 times, so provenance survives the cleaner.",
    "what_we_do": "Ten of our twelve paragraphs are 527-984-word walls of consecutive fact sentences with no ordering logic (our Introduction hops displacement theory → 4IR definition → firm study → 1880s history → WEF forecast → IDC spending → productivity paradox → ImageNet error rates → tourism → MENA in ONE paragraph), and 16 sentences hang on anaphora whose antecedent is a deleted [n] marker — our Generative AI section OPENS with a result attributed to a study never named.",
    "quote_them": "Consistent with this, Han et al. show that AI investment (proxied by AI-skill demands in postings) can increase **firm resilience** to natural disaster shocks, but realized productivity benefits are constrained by a lack of complementary organizational designs [[94]](https://pubsonline.informs.org/doi/10.1287/isre.2022.0440). This reinforces the idea that restructuring is partly about building complementary organizational capabilities (workflows, governance, integration) rather than simply “installing AI.”",
    "quote_us": "The study also documents a 1.2 percentage point decrease in the probability of receiving any employment in a given month—approximately a 10% drop relative to baseline—and a 4.7% reduction in the number of jobs conditional on being employed.[38]",
    "criterion": "READABILITY (+0.143 gap) and COMPREHENSIVENESS 'evidence' — the cleaner deletes [38], leaving 'The study' with no referent for the entire section",
    "why_it_scores": "After RACE strips markers, bodhi's sentence still says WHO found it (Han et al.) and WHY it matters (the implication clause). Our sentence becomes an unattributed orphan: 'The study also documents...' — which study? The judge reads unattributed numbers as weaker evidence, and 677-word walls poison every dimension via cross-dimension bleed."
   },
   {
    "what_they_do": "bodhi opens with a scope-and-definitions block that explicitly restates and ENFORCES the prompt's constraint ('only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles'), defines 'restructuring' in five enumerated dimensions, and states its evidence-handling rule — then repeats the constraint when it bites ('Note on evidence limitation... claims must remain at the level of documented research focus').",
    "what_we_do": "We never acknowledge the journal-articles-only instruction and then violate it in prose, citing WEF, IDC, PwC/IBM/Deloitte/Gartner, Brookings, Goldman Sachs, BLS, and 'Morgan' as evidence.",
    "quote_them": "**Evidence constraint**: I only cite journal articles. Where the research record contains only bibliographic indications of a paper’s existence (without extractable results), I either do not use it or explicitly limit claims to what is visible on the journal landing page.",
    "quote_us": "Economic analyses by PwC, IBM, Deloitte, and Gartner Research suggest that adoption of AI will increase global GDP by 15 percent by 2030, representing an additional $15.7 trillion.[8]",
    "criterion": "INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING (+0.115 gap, weight 0.25) — the prompt's one hard constraint is 'Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles'",
    "why_it_scores": "bodhi converts the prompt's constraint into visible, checkable behavior the judge can verify in the first 100 words, and re-invokes it whenever evidence is thin. We silently break the single explicit instruction with consultancy and bank forecasts presented as literature. This is the cheapest criterion on the board to fix: a scope block plus source filtering."
   },
   {
    "what_they_do": "bodhi appraises the METHODS, not just the findings: sections 2.1-2.10 give each measurement strategy a bolded Strength/Limitation pair (9 pairs), and section 8.3 enumerates five named open research problems ('Measurement drift', 'Causal identification of labor-market impacts', 'General equilibrium effects', 'Quality-of-work outcomes', 'Sector coverage') — what the evidence CANNOT yet resolve, tied back to specific cited studies.",
    "what_we_do": "Our Limitations section describes our own pipeline's telemetry — source-tier percentages and an internal 'telemetry block' — leaking machine internals instead of appraising the literature, and our research-gaps prose is generic.",
    "quote_them": "**Strength**: high-frequency, establishment-level behavior; can observe shifting skill demands in real time.  \n**Limitation**: postings reflect intended hiring, not realized employment; AI vacancy postings may understate embedded AI use that doesn’t require specialized hiring.",
    "quote_us": "The telemetry block does not surface an explicit date range for the corpus, which limits the ability to characterize the evidence horizon or identify temporal gaps in source coverage.",
    "criterion": "INSIGHT — 'Critical Scrutiny of Limitations' and 'forward-looking thinking, can anticipate trends'; COMPREHENSIVENESS depth",
    "why_it_scores": "bodhi's limitations are limitations OF THE EVIDENCE (feasibility ≠ adoption; postings ≠ realized employment) — precisely the rubric's 'critical scrutiny'. Ours are limitations of our software, in vocabulary ('pipeline', 'telemetry block', 'T1/T4/T6 tiers') that reveals to the judge it is reading machine output. Strength/Limitation pairs are also digit-free, proper-noun-free sentences our INTERPRETATION lane could ship today."
   },
   {
    "what_they_do": "bodhi ships zero pipeline artifacts — its prose is clean of any generation-machinery residue; even its one meta-statement ('I only cite journal articles') is in-genre reviewer voice.",
    "what_we_do": "Our body text contains 25 literal '(also mirrored)' debris strings inline after citations, plus two outright sentence fragments — one of which OPENS our flagship 'Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions' section — and an unresolved 'the company' referring to ALMATO before ALMATO is introduced.",
    "quote_them": "A dominant framing treats occupations as **bundles of tasks**, with AI affecting subsets of tasks rather than “automating entire jobs.”",
    "quote_us": "Bureau of Labor Statistics data or projections for the idea of a general acceleration of job loss or a structural break with trends pre-dating the AI revolution.[70] Using more conservative specifications with agent and tenure fixed effects, the deployment of AI increased resolutions per hour by 0.30 chats, or 13.8%, from an average of 2.12.[71] (also mirrored)",
    "criterion": "READABILITY 'Language Expression Quality' + cross-dimension bleed — the judge writes its comparative narrative BEFORE scoring, and 25 debris strings and a fragment heading the synthesis section anchor that narrative",
    "why_it_scores": "The judge's forced chain-of-thought will note '(also mirrored)' x25 and a verbless fragment opening our synthesis section, and that narrative sits in context while it scores insight and comprehensiveness (the other 86%). This is pure self-inflicted loss: a deterministic report-to-report scrub (zero generation noise, per the brief's measurement section) removes it for free."
   }
  ]
 },
 {
  "findings": [
   {
    "what_they_do": "cellcog-max never lets a pipeline artifact reach the page. Every sentence is finished prose. Its 16,334 words contain zero editorial residue, zero internal tags, zero fragments.",

It appears in the Theoretical Foundations section (14x), the Cross-Study Synthesis section (9x), and the Conclusions (5x). RACE's citation cleaner strips [n] markers and the bibliography, but it does NOT strip this — it is plain English inside the sentence. The judge, reading article_1 side by side with a clean reference, sees a machine's scratch-pad. This is the single most damaging line-level defect in the report and it is invisible in every proxy metric we have measured (words, H3, paragraph length, bullets).",
    "quote_them": "The German pattern is not accidental; it reflects coordinated bargaining, works councils, and the dual vocational-education system that together transform what a U.S. commuting-zone framing would record as displacement into within-firm reallocation.",

'(also mirrored)' x25 hands it a one-line thesis for that narrative: 'Article 1 appears to be unedited machine output with internal processing artifacts left in the text.'     "criterion": "Readability — 'Overall Article Structure and Logical Flow' (the largest readability criterion); Insight — 'clear logical reasoning'",

   },
   {
    "what_they_do": "cellcog attaches a named author, a named journal, an exact N, and an identification strategy to essentially every empirical claim.     "what_we_do": "After citation stripping, our facts become unattributed assertions. Our prose says 'One study...', 'A separate review...', 'That study...' (4x), 'One analysis...' (3x), 'A comprehensive review...', 'Researchers find...', 'one source reports...'. We name a journal FIVE times in 9,200 words. The prompt says 'Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles' — and the judge cannot see a single citation. It can only see whether the prose SOUNDS like it is built on journal articles. Ours does not.",

This is the mechanism by which cellcog's extra length BUYS comprehensiveness: the marginal words are not padding, they are named-venue + N + identification-strategy attached to each claim. Every fact becomes checkable-looking. That is literally the 'data and factual support' criterion."
   },
   {
    "what_they_do": "cellcog's synthesis is adjudication, not enumeration. It sets two conflicting well-identified findings against each other, then RULES on how they relate — a sentence that carries no new facts but does all the intellectual work. It does this dozens of times, and dedicates a whole section (Section 5, eight numbered syntheses) to it.",

The judge does not have to infer our weakness — we told it. Deleting this one sentence costs nothing and removes the judge's best evidence against us."
   },
   {

    "quote_them": "Three epistemic caveats attach to the peer-reviewed constraint. First, publication bias is not merely hypothetical: Guarascio et al. (2025) and Jurkat et al.    },
   {
    "what_they_do": "cellcog reserves an entire section for claims it OWNS, and it tags each one with its epistemic status so the judge can see exactly where evidence ends and argument begins. This is the 'original insight' criterion, executed literally and with a compliance mechanism that keeps it faithful.",

The reference does the same with its Section 9.3.",
    "what_we_do": "Our 'Conclusions and Research Gaps' contains no gaps we identified — only gaps other authors reported, and a paragraph about AI-generated literature reviews being rejected by journals, which is not about the labor market at all.",
    "quote_them": "Five frontier questions merit peer-reviewed attention. First, will the displacement-augmentation balance documented for GPT-4-class models hold as reasoning capability scales toward GPT-5 and beyond? Second, if the short-run skill compression of Section 5.3 persists, what happens to returns to human-capital accumulation and to firms' incentive to invest in training?",
    "quote_us": "The growing integration of AI into academic research workflows itself poses challenges, as AI-generated literature reviews are increasingly being rejected for lacking genuine academic analysis, with journals tightening standards around AI-assisted writing.[91]",
    "criterion": "Insight — 'forward-looking thinking, can anticipate trends'; 'identifies key issues... provides insightful solutions'",
    "why_it_scores": "This is the worst sentence in the report. In a literature review that IS an AI-generated literature review, we inserted an off-topic, blog-sourced claim that AI-generated literature reviews are being rejected for lacking genuine academic analysis. A comparative judge writing its analysis BEFORE scoring is being handed the verdict, pre-written, by article_1 itself. THE FACTS THAT MUST SHAPE THE DESIGN:
- We are 0.4382. bodhi wins task 72 at 0.5441 **on 4,361 words — 44% SHORTER than us.** LENGTH IS DEAD.
- Our READABILITY (0.3774) is the WORST SCORE ON THE ENTIRE BOARD — below grok and perplexity, whom we outrank.
  Cause: 12 paragraphs, median 677 words, 0 subsections. Every other system: 38-170 word paragraphs.

  naming authors/venues IN PROSE.
- Measurement: judge SD 0.0074; k=5 paired resolves +0.0094 at 2 sigma. A report->report transform of a banked
  artifact has ZERO generation noise — cheapest, most sensitive arm available.

Design the plan. Ground EVERY lever in a verbatim rubric criterion or a measured artifact fact. We lost an entire
night to proxy metrics — do not hand us another. Where uncertain, SEARCH (papers, GitHub, the artifacts at /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board).

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# BRIEF V2 — EVERYTHING WE NOW KNOW. Design the plan that takes POLARIS to the top of the board.
2	# (Every number here is MEASURED from primary artifacts on disk or read from the grader's own source code.)
3	
4	## 1. THE BOARD (task 72, OUR evaluator = GPT-5.5, per-task scores from the benchmark's own raw_results)
5	| system | OVERALL | compreh | INSIGHT | instr-follow | readability | words | H3 | med-para | bullets |
6	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
7	| **bodhi** (task-72 winner) | **0.5441** | 0.5441 | 0.5457 | 0.5559 | 0.5199 | **4,361** | 40 | 59w | 14 |
8	| lunon | 0.5406 | 0.5437 | 0.5594 | 0.5323 | 0.5037 | 11,476 | 50 | 71w | 11 |
9	| WhaleCloud | 0.5396 | 0.5417 | 0.5429 | 0.5393 | 0.5284 | 7,892 | 33 | 63w | 26 |
10	| dalpha | 0.5252 | 0.5213 | 0.5200 | 0.5350 | 0.5284 | 4,039 | 0 | 38w | 0 |
11	| sourcery | 0.5205 | 0.5211 | 0.5397 | 0.5033 | 0.5035 | 9,849 | 38 | 66w | 106 |
12	| **gemini-2.5-pro-DR = THE REFERENCE** | 0.5102 | 0.5111 | 0.5035 | 0.5187 | 0.5100 | 7,907 | 0 | 131w | 61 |
13	| openai-DR | 0.4743 | 0.4789 | 0.4475 | 0.4982 | 0.4859 | 8,513 | 0 | 170w | 13 |
14	| **POLARIS (rank10)** | **0.4382** | 0.4549 | 0.4238 | 0.4409 | **0.3774** | 7,742 | **0** | **677w** | 0 |
15	| grok | 0.4316 | 0.4292 | 0.3873 | 0.4670 | 0.4706 | 1,060 | 0 | 72w | 0 |
16	| perplexity | 0.4241 | 0.4228 | 0.4015 | 0.4391 | 0.4507 | 1,510 | 0 | 58w | 0 |
17	(cellcog-max = 0.5578 corpus-wide #1; it publishes no per-task breakdown. Its task-72 artifact: 16,334w, 31 H3, 75w paras, 0 bullets.)
18	**"ADORE 52.65" IS FICTION** — absent from the backing data of both boards. Do not plan against it.
19	
20	## 2. THE GAP, DECOMPOSED (us -> bodhi)
21	| dim | gap | weight | overall |
22	|---|---|---|---|
23	| INSIGHT | +0.122 | 0.32 | **+0.039** |
24	| instruction-following | +0.115 | 0.25 | +0.029 |
25	| comprehensiveness | +0.089 | 0.29 | +0.026 |
26	| readability | **+0.143** | 0.14 | +0.020 |
27	| | | | **= +0.114 -> 0.552** |
28	**OUR READABILITY (0.3774) IS THE WORST SCORE ON THE ENTIRE BOARD** — below grok and perplexity, the two systems we beat.
29	
30	## 3. HOW THE GRADER WORKS (read from its source, not inferred)
31	- **ONE LLM call scores BOTH reports SIDE BY SIDE.** Ours = article_1, reference = article_2, always. (race.py:99-104)
32	- **0-10 per criterion**, ~25 criteria. Bands: 4-6 "Average", **6-8 "Good"**, **8-10 "Excellent"**.
33	  We are being read at ~6.6; the reference reads ~9.0. **To beat parity a criterion must cross from "Good" into "Excellent". There is no credit for being a better 7.**
34	- **Overall = ours/(ours+reference)** — COMPRESSIVE. +1.0 raw point on EVERY criterion = only **+0.036 overall**.
35	- **THE JUDGE WRITES A COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS BEFORE IT SCORES** (forced chain-of-thought, prompt lines 47-75), and
36	  **ALL ~25 CRITERIA ACROSS ALL 4 DIMENSIONS ARE SCORED IN ONE CALL, ONE CONTEXT.** Nothing firewalls the dimensions.
37	  **=> CROSS-DIMENSION BLEED IS REAL AND UNINSTRUCTED.** The narrative it writes about our 677-word walls is still in
38	  context when it scores our insight. Our readability disaster is taxing the other 86% of the score.
39	- **Dimension weights (100-task mean): insight 0.352 | comprehensiveness 0.292 | instruction-following 0.215 | readability 0.141.**
40	  Insight + comprehensiveness = **64% of the score**.
41	- **LENGTH IS NEVER MENTIONED IN THE SCORING PROMPT.** No criterion rewards it. Padding is penalised at only ~0.7% of
42	  overall, while substance-bearing expansion pays into 64%. => "longer but DENSER" wins; "longer and thinner" is pointless.
43	- **EXECUTIVE SUMMARIES ARE NOT REWARDED.** Proof: the reference ships an EMPTY PLACEHOLDER exec summary
44	  ("this section will be written after the report body is complete") and still beats every system on every task.
45	- **HEDGING IS NOT REWARDED.** No criterion anywhere pays for softness or uncertainty-as-stance. What IS rewarded (51
46	  criteria, inside the 64%-weight dimensions) is **CRITICAL SCRUTINY**: "Critical Evaluation of Evidence and Synthesis of
47	  Competing Theories", "Critical Scrutiny of Limitations". **The judge does not want us to sound uncertain. It wants us to ADJUDICATE.**
48	- INSIGHT rubric, verbatim: "deep analysis and original insights rather than simply repeating known information";
49	  "clear logical reasoning and effectively explains causal relationships behind phenomena"; "identifies key issues ...
50	  provides insightful solutions"; "forward-looking thinking, can anticipate trends".
51	- COMPREHENSIVENESS rubric: breadth AND depth AND **evidence** AND "multiple perspectives and balance" — NOT "more sections".
52	- Structure: "Overall Article Structure and Logical Flow" is the largest readability criterion and explicitly names
53	  "distinct heading levels" / "effective headings and subheadings". Tables/charts: max ~2.8% of overall. Worth having, not worth optimising hard.
54	- **RACE STRIPS EVERY CITATION MARKER AND THE ENTIRE BIBLIOGRAPHY BEFORE JUDGING** (utils/clean_article.py, an LLM
55	  cleaner). VERIFIED: our 9,300 words -> 7,692 read; 345 [n] markers -> 0; 105-entry bibliography -> DELETED.
56	  **=> ALL bibliography/citation-quality work scores ZERO. Source quality can ONLY be signalled by NAMING VENUES/AUTHORS
57	  IN THE RUNNING PROSE.** (The 0.5102 reference ships ZERO inline citations.)
58	
59	## 4. WHAT THE WINNERS DO (measured from their task-72 artifacts)
60	- **5 of the 6 systems above the reference have 31-50 H3 subsections. Every system at/below the reference has ZERO.**
61	  (dalpha is the lone exception: 0 H3, but 38-word paragraphs.) **We have 0 H3 and 677-word paragraphs.**
62	- Winners' median paragraph: **59-75 words**. Reference: 131. OpenAI: 170. **Us: 677 — a different regime entirely.**
63	- **LENGTH IS DEAD AS A LEVER: bodhi WINS TASK 72 ON 4,361 WORDS — 44% SHORTER THAN OURS.** dalpha scores 0.5252 on 4,039.
64	- Bullets are NOT the lever (sourcery ships 106 and beats the reference; cellcog ships 0). Tables are NOT the lever (0-5).
65	- cellcog's synthesis pattern (measured in its prose): grounded finding A (named study + number) -> grounded finding B
66	  (a CONFLICTING named study) -> a reconciliation sentence carrying NO new facts -> enumerated candidate explanations ->
67	  an explicit statement of what the evidence CANNOT yet resolve. Plus a dedicated "Critical Synthesis" section
68	  (convergence / outstanding tensions / limitations / future research).
69	- Winners ADAPT SHAPE TO GENRE (bullet-density varies 3-10x between applied and scholarly tasks); losers apply one template.
70	
71	## 5. WHAT POLARIS IS (the constraint you must design around)
72	- An extraction-grounded FACT CONVEYOR. `verify_sentence_provenance` (provenance_generator.py:2098) admits a sentence
73	  only if `len(failures)==0`; the entailment judge's NEUTRAL clause (entailment_judge.py:588) kills any sentence that
74	  "introduces a fact, entity, **MECHANISM** ... NOT present in the SPAN".
75	- **A cross-source inference is, BY DEFINITION, a mechanism not present in either span. Our own gate deletes exactly the
76	  sentence type that earns INSIGHT (0.32 — the heaviest weight, and our worst dimension).**
77	- Boundary, precisely: a CONJUNCTION of two sources ("X found 2.1% [a] while Y found 0.4% [b]") PASSES. Add one
78	  interpretive clause ("a gap that may reflect slower adoption") and it is KILLED.
79	- **FAITHFULNESS IS THE MOAT AND IS NOT FOR SALE.** No fabricated fact, number, quote or attribution may ever ship. Any
80	  design that can leak one is unshippable, whatever it scores. We have a working deterministic contract
81	  (scripts/reflow_report.py): FACT (span-verified, byte-identical) / STRUCTURE (assembled from sidecar data) /
82	  INTERPRETATION (no digits, no new proper nouns, no citations, must sit beside >=2 facts from >=2 sources,
83	  contradiction-screened fail-closed). **NOTE: it was built around HEDGING — and the rubric does not reward hedging.
84	  It rewards ADJUDICATION. That contract needs rethinking, not just retuning.**
85	
86	## 6. MEASUREMENT (fixed tonight — use it, never score n=1 again)
87	- Judge noise: **SD = 0.0074** (byte-identical artifact scored 6x). Baseline is tighter (SD 0.0020).
88	- k=5 paired => SE(diff) = 0.0047 => **smallest resolvable effect = +0.0094 at 2 sigma.**
89	- Generation noise (re-composing the same config) is LARGER (~0.016) and applies ONLY to arms needing a new compose.
90	  A report->report transform of a BANKED artifact has ZERO generation noise — cheap AND sensitive. Prefer it.
91	- Our own history: baseline 0.4062 -> rank10 0.4382 = **+0.032, z=10** (real). The depth levers DID work, modestly.
92	  But every subsequent lever (padding removal, source firewall) scored FLAT. And ALL of it was aimed at proxies.
93	
94	## 7. YOUR TASK
95	Design the plan that takes POLARIS from **0.4382** to **the top of this board (0.54-0.56)**.
96	- Ground every lever in the rubric text or a measured artifact. NO PROXIES — we lost a night to them.
97	- State, for each lever: the criterion it moves, the expected points, how it FAILS, and the cheap test that proves the
98	  mechanism fired BEFORE we pay for a 65-minute compose.
99	- Respect the faithfulness moat absolutely. If a lever needs ungrounded prose, say so explicitly and justify the exact
100	  containment.
101	- Be honest about the ceiling. If your plan does not reach 0.54, SAY SO and name the residual.
102	- Where you are UNCERTAIN, SEARCH (papers, GitHub, the artifacts on disk at
103	  /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/). Do not guess.
104

TASK 72 PROMPT:
Please write a literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the labor market. Focus on how AI, as a key driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, is causing significant disruptions and affecting various industries. Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles.

DIMENSION WEIGHTS: {"readability": 0.14, "insight": 0.32, "comprehensiveness": 0.29, "instruction_following": 0.25}

=== COMPREHENSIVENESS (weight 0.29) ===
- [w=0.10] Grounding in AI and the Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) Context :: Assesses whether the review adequately defines AI within the context of the 4IR and explains its fundamental role as a driver of labor market restructuring. This contextual framing is crucial for understanding the scope and nature of the impacts discussed.
- [w=0.25] Breadth of Labor Market Restructuring Dimensions Covered :: Evaluates the extent to which the review addresses the multifaceted nature of AI's impact on the labor market, including job creation, job displacement, job transformation, changes in skill demands, wage effects, and productivity changes.
- [w=0.25] Scope of Industry-Specific Analysis :: Checks if the review examines the restructuring impact of AI across a diverse range of relevant industries, highlighting both common trends and sector-specific nuances. The task explicitly requires focusing on 'various industries.'
- [w=0.15] Exploration of AI's Disruptive Character and Scale :: Assesses whether the review sufficiently explores the 'significant disruptions' caused by AI, including the magnitude, speed, and transformative potential of these changes on labor market structures and dynamics.
- [w=0.15] Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized :: Evaluates if the review synthesizes a broad and representative selection of current, high-quality academic literature (from the specified journal articles), covering the main research themes, findings, and ongoing debates regarding AI's impact on the labor market. This ensures the review's content is built upon a solid and comprehensive foundation of existing knowledge.
- [w=0.10] Balanced Discussion of AI's Labor Market Impacts :: Assesses whether the review presents a nuanced perspective by discussing both the challenges (e.g., displacement, skill gaps, inequality) and opportunities (e.g., new job creation, productivity gains, enhanced work quality) arising from AI's influence on the labor market.

=== INSIGHT (weight 0.32) ===
- [w=0.25] Analytical Depth in Characterizing AI-Driven Labor Market Restructuring Mechanisms :: Assesses if the review moves beyond superficial descriptions of labor market changes to deeply analyze the underlying *mechanisms* (e.g., task automation, skill augmentation, job creation/destruction dynamics, organizational adaptation) through which AI specifically reshapes job roles, skill requirements, and overall labor market structures.
- [w=0.25] Critical Synthesis and Nuanced Evaluation of AI's Disruptive Impacts Across Industries :: Evaluates the review's ability to critically synthesize diverse findings from high-quality journal articles to present a nuanced understanding of AI's 'significant disruptions.' This includes identifying patterns of impact, variations across different industries, and areas of consensus, debate, or uncertainty in the literature, rather than merely cataloging effects.
- [w=0.15] Insightful Integration of AI's Role within the Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) Context :: Measures how effectively the review articulates and leverages the 4IR framework to provide a deeper, more contextualized understanding of the *nature, scale, and interconnectedness* of AI's transformative power on the labor market, rather than treating AI's impact in isolation or merely mentioning the 4IR superficially.
- [w=0.20] Identification and Articulation of Emergent Themes, Theoretical Linkages, or Novel Perspectives :: Assesses whether the review transcends simple summarization to identify and clearly articulate overarching emergent themes, significant theoretical linkages between concepts, or potentially novel perspectives derived from the synthesis of the reviewed literature on AI's labor market impact.
- [w=0.15] Value and Foresight in Delineating Implications and Future Research Agendas :: Evaluates the review's capacity to distill strategically valuable implications (e.g., for policy, education, workforce adaptation strategies) and to clearly delineate critical research gaps or promising future research agendas based on the comprehensive synthesis of the current state of knowledge.

=== INSTRUCTION_FOLLOWING (weight 0.25) ===
- [w=0.10] Adherence to 'Literature Review' Format and Purpose :: Assesses if the article is structured and executed as a literature review, meaning it synthesizes and discusses existing published research on the topic, rather than presenting new empirical findings or being solely an opinion piece.
- [w=0.20] Consistent Focus on 'Restructuring Impact of AI on the Labor Market' :: Evaluates whether the article's content remains consistently and primarily focused on AI's restructuring effects on the labor market, as per the core task instruction, without significant digressions into unrelated AI applications or general economic theory not tied to labor impacts.
- [w=0.15] Integration of 'AI as a Key Driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution' Theme :: Checks if the review explicitly incorporates and discusses AI within the specific context of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, as mandated by the task's focus requirements.
- [w=0.15] Explicit Addressal of AI-Driven 'Significant Disruptions' in the Labor Market :: Assesses whether the review specifically addresses and elaborates on the 'significant disruptions' caused by AI in the labor market, fulfilling another key focus point of the task.
- [w=0.15] Coverage of AI's Impact on 'Various Industries' :: Evaluates if the review discusses the impact of AI on the labor market across a spectrum of 'various industries,' rather than limiting its scope to a single industry or providing only a generic overview, as per the instruction.
- [w=0.15] Exclusive Citation of 'High-Quality Journal Articles' :: Verifies that all cited sources are academic, peer-reviewed journal articles (or meet a clear standard of 'high-quality' if defined contextually, e.g., leading journals), and that other forms of literature (e.g., books, conference proceedings, news articles, blogs, non-peer-reviewed reports) are not cited, adhering to the 'only cites high-quality... journal articles' constraint.
- [w=0.10] Exclusive Citation of 'English-Language' Journal Articles :: Ensures that all journal articles cited in the review are originally published in English or are established English translations, strictly adhering to the language constraint for sources.

=== READABILITY (weight 0.14) ===
- [w=0.20] L1: Language Clarity, Precision, and Academic Tone :: Evaluates the accuracy and fluency of sentence construction, grammar, spelling, and punctuation. Assesses the precise, consistent, and appropriate use of technical terminology related to AI, labor markets, the Fourth Industrial Revolution, and specific industries. Ensures an objective, formal academic tone suitable for a literature review. For example, terms like 'skill-biased technical change' or specific AI techniques should be used correctly and, if necessary, briefly contextualized for a broader academic audience.
- [w=0.20] S1: Overall Structure and Logical Organization :: Assesses the coherence and logic of the review's macro-structure. This includes a clear introduction defining the review's scope, purpose, and organizational map; logically sequenced thematic sections with informative headings that reflect the facets of AI's impact on labor (e.g., by industry, skill level, type of disruption); and a conclusion that effectively synthesizes the main themes from the literature and perhaps points to gaps or future research directions.
- [w=0.15] S2: Paragraph Cohesion and Transitions :: Evaluates if each paragraph focuses on a single, clear idea or theme derived from the literature, with sentences flowing logically within the paragraph. Assesses the effectiveness of transitional phrases, sentences, and logical connectors in linking ideas smoothly between paragraphs and across different sections of the review, ensuring a coherent narrative thread.
- [w=0.15] P1: Clarity and Synthesis in Presenting Sourced Information :: Assesses how clearly, concisely, and accurately key arguments, findings, theories, and methodologies from the cited journal articles are presented. Evaluates the effectiveness of synthesizing information from multiple sources to build a coherent understanding of topics (e.g., AI's effect on job displacement vs. creation, or changes in skill demands across industries), rather than just serially summarizing individual papers. Checks for appropriate information density and avoidance of redundancy.
- [w=0.10] D1: Clarity of Data/Evidence Referenced or Summarized :: Evaluates the clarity with which quantitative or qualitative data, empirical findings, or specific evidence from cited studies (e.g., statistics on automation rates, survey results on worker sentiment) are described, interpreted, or summarized within the review. If the review itself uses tables or figures to compile or synthesize information from sources (e.g., a summary table of key studies and their findings), assesses their clarity, labeling, and effectiveness in aiding comprehension.
- [w=0.10] F1: Formatting, Layout, and Visual Consistency :: Evaluates the use of consistent and professional formatting (e.g., font choice and size, spacing, margins, heading styles) that enhances readability. Assesses the overall visual layout for ease of navigation and reduced reader fatigue, including clear paragraph breaks. Also considers the visual consistency and unobtrusive integration of in-text citations and the clarity of the bibliography's formatting, which contributes to the academic presentation.
- [w=0.10] A1: Audience Adaptation and Explanation of Terms :: Assesses if the language, level of detail, and style of argumentation are appropriate for an academic audience interested in AI's impact on the labor market. Evaluates whether potentially ambiguous or highly specialized terms are sufficiently contextualized or briefly explained to ensure understanding by readers who may not be experts in every specific sub-domain discussed (e.g., specific AI algorithms or niche economic theories).

input.diagnosis: We are a fact conveyor scored by an adjudication rubric. Mechanism, in order of causal weight: (1) Our own gates delete the scoring sentence class. verify_sentence_provenance + the entailment judge's NEUTRAL clause kill any sentence introducing a mechanism not in a span, and the class-I contract in reflow_report.py additionally REQUIRES a hedge lexeme and caps interpretation at 8% of sentences. Result: 97.8% of our sentences are citation-terminated reported facts. INSIGHT (w=0.32, our 0.4238) pays verbatim for 'Critical Synthesis and Nuanced Evaluation... rather than merely cataloging effects' and 'Emergent Themes, Theoretical Linkages, or Novel Perspectives' — bodhi spends ~47% of its sentences on exactly that (labeled, digit-free, no-new-noun implication/reconciliation sentences), we spend ~2%. (2) RACE strips every [n] marker and the whole bibliography before judging, so our ONLY provenance channel is deleted; our facts become unattributed orphans ('The study also documents...') while bodhi names authors in prose 54 times. Source quality survives only as prose register — and our surviving register includes vendor CAGRs and blog statistics, a visible violation of the prompt's one hard constraint ('only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles', w=0.15 of instruction-following). (3) The judge scores all ~25 criteria in ONE call and writes a comparative narrative FIRST; our 677-word median paragraphs (worst readability on the board, 0.3774), zero H3s, '(also mirrored)' debris, fragments, and two written confessions ('No contradictions were detected by the pipeline', the T1/T4/T6 telemetry Limitations) hand that narrative its thesis — 'article_1 is unedited machine output that performed no synthesis' — which then taxes the other 86% of the score via bleed. bodhi wins on 4,361 words because each marginal word is a named rubric criterion (attribution, mechanism, adjudication, claim-first heading); each of our marginal words is another orphan statistic. Length is dead; criterion-coverage density is the variable.

Mitigations: screen only checks CONTRADICTION not entailment (already the design); vacuity check = ruling sentence must share content stems with BOTH premise facts; label rotation

input.faithfulness_contract: REVISED CONTRACT — from hedged gloss to contained adjudication. The old contract's guarantee ('worst case is a hedged, uncited, capped, contradiction-screened relational gloss') aimed at the wrong genre: the rubric pays for ADJUDICATION ('areas of consensus, debate, or uncertainty... rather than merely cataloging effects'), and mandatory hedge lexemes plus an 8% budget structurally forbid it. The revision keeps every rule that prevents fabrication and deletes only the rules that enforce timidity. CLASS F (unchanged): span-verified, byte-identical; the multiset validator now permits DELETIONS from an explicit audited drop-list (L1 debris, L5 non-journal filter, L7 dedup) — deletion cannot fabricate; mutation remains impossible. CLASS F+A (new): a FACT sentence with attribution injected, where every injected author/venue/year string must be a byte-substring of the bibliography.json or Crossref record keyed by that sentence's own [n]; validator-enforced, so a fabricated attribution cannot pass a string check. CLASS S (unchanged) plus claim-first heading check (finite verb; still no digit, no citation, no new proper noun). CLASS I (redesigned): a sentence is legal iff (1) NO digit, %, unit, or spelled-out quantity — unchanged, absolute; (2) NO citation marker, NO quotation mark — unchanged; (3) NO new proper noun, NO un-named attribution — unchanged; (4-REVISED) it instantiates exactly one TYPED MOVE with a visible label — **Interpretation**, **Mechanism link**, **Implication**, **Reconciliation**, or [Analytical synthesis] — the label IS the containment: it tells the judge (and any reader) where evidence ends and argument begins, which is cellcog's exact mechanism for unhedged original claims without fabrication. Hedge lexemes are permitted, never required. Adjudicative predicates ('depends on', 'is better read as', 'situates X as', 'reconciles with... if') are legal. Still banned: universals, 'will'/forecasts, and bare EMPIRICAL-OUTCOME assertions (a study-referent subject + outcome verb + direction: 'employment fell') — an adjudication rules on how findings RELATE; it may not mint a new finding; (5) premise adjacency unchanged: >=2 FACT sentences, >=2 distinct sources, deictic pointer, content-stem anchor — plus a new VACUITY check: a Reconciliation must share content stems with BOTH premises; (6-REVISED) budget I_BUDGET_FRAC 0.08 -> 0.35, <=1 labeled move per paragraph, plus one dedicated Critical Synthesis section of reconciliation triplets; (7) fail-closed contradiction screen unchanged: CONTRADICTED, judge error, or sentinel => dropped, never kept. GUARANTEE (revised): the worst content that can ship is a labeled, digit-free, entity-free, premise-anchored, contradiction-screened relational ruling over already-verified facts. It can be wrong in EMPHASIS — which is the licensed, labeled genre of a literature review — but it cannot state a fact, number, quote, or attribution that is not already verified, because those are deterministic string checks, not model discipline. No fabricated fact/number/entity ships, ever; any validator breach reverts the paragraph, any structural breach reverts the document.
input.expected_total: Honest sum: nominal lever sum is +0.095 to +0.124 (L1 .005-.010, L2 .017, L3 .015-.020, L4 .028-.034, L5 .020, L6 .005-.010, L7 .003-.008, L8 .002-.005), which naively reaches 0.53-0.56. But the levers double-count: L2's bleed relief is partly what L4's insight estimate assumes, and the compressive overall (ours/(ours+ref)) shrinks late gains. Applying a ~25-30% overlap haircut: realistic landing is 0.51-0.53 from report->report transforms alone — SHORT of bodhi's 0.5441 by roughly 0.01-0.03. That is still rank ~4-6 on this board (past gemini-reference 0.5102, into the dalpha/sourcery band). The residual is named in ceiling_honesty: it is corpus quality, not report form, and cannot be transformed in. Reaching 0.54-0.56 requires one additional large arm — a journal-first retrieval rebuild feeding these same transforms — which carries generation noise (~0.016) and a 65-min compose, and should only be paid for AFTER the transform stack has been measured to its plateau.
input.kill_rule: Every lever is a report->report transform of the banked rank10 artifact (zero generation noise) and is scored k=5 paired against the previous stage with the exact RACE harness (SD 0.0074; smallest resolvable effect +0.0094 at 2 sigma). KILL a lever if: (a) it fails its mechanical cheap test — no judge calls are spent until the mechanism is PROVEN to have fired in the artifact text; (b) after passing the mechanical test, two design iterations both score <+0.0094 at k=5 paired — the lever is dead, revert, move on (no third retune; that is how we lost a night to proxies); (c) any validator run ever finds a fabricated fact, number, entity, or attribution in shippable output — immediate permanent kill of that lever's mechanism, full revert, no retuning, regardless of score. Corollary: no lever may ever be declared successful on a proxy metric (word count, H3 count, uncited-share) — proxies gate spending, only paired judge deltas bank progress. Composes (65 min, generation noise 0.016) are paid for only after the transform stack plateaus on two consecutive flat levers.
input.ceiling_honesty: Three things this plan cannot do. (1) It cannot manufacture journal-grade evidence. The corpus behind the banked artifact has 17 T1 + 12 T2 sources out of 105, venue metadata for 32, DOIs for 26. cellcog's comprehensiveness ('Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized', w=0.15) is bought with QJE/AER/Econometrica papers carrying exact Ns and identification strategies; bodhi's instruction-following is bought by actually citing only journals. A transform can filter our blogs OUT and name our real journals IN, but it cannot add the ~40 named-venue empirical anchors we never retrieved — that is the 0.01-0.03 residual, and closing it requires a journal-first retrieval + recompose arm (large effort, generation noise, only worth paying for after the transform plateau). (2) It cannot guarantee the adjudication layer fills its budget. The fail-closed contradiction screen keeps faithfulness absolute, but a true reconciliation names a mechanism absent from both premises — the same property that made the old NEUTRAL clause lethal. If the screen (or the vacuity check) kills >50% of candidate rulings, insight gains land at the low end (+0.02 not +0.034); the drop-rate log in L4's cheap test detects this before we pay for scoring. (3) It cannot beat the compressive scoring function's arithmetic: overall = ours/(ours+reference), so even crossing several criteria from 'Good' into 'Excellent' yields diminishing overall returns, and cross-dimension bleed relief — our largest hidden asset — is real but unquantifiable in advance. Stated plainly: this plan very likely takes 0.4382 to 0.51-0.53 on transforms alone (past the gemini reference, top-half of the board) and reaches the 0.54-0.56 summit only if the journal-first corpus arm also lands. Anyone who promises 0.56 from report-shape alone is selling the same proxy night we already lost.
